Surrogate oscillation scores (shuffle or trend-preserving)

Source: R/oscore.R
oscillation_score_surrogates.Rd

Generates surrogate signals and computes oscillation scores for reference distributions. Fixes edge cases from the MATLAB implementation (e.g., negative p-values, missing sums).

Usage

oscillation_score_surrogates(
  signal,
  fs,
  flim,
  nrep,
  fpeak,
  keep_trend = FALSE,
  trend_dist = c("gamma"),
  trend_ddt = NULL,
  trend_alpha = 0.05,
  surrogate_method = c("auto", "event_shuffle", "event_trend", "phase_randomized"),
  signal_mode = c("auto", "event", "continuous"),
  warnings = TRUE,
  fcor = FALSE,
  taper = c("none", "hann", "hanning")
)

Arguments

signal

numeric vector.

fs

sampling rate in Hz.

flim

length-2 numeric vector c(fmin, fmax).

nrep

number of surrogates.

fpeak

peak frequency of original signal (Hz); required for windowing.

keep_trend

logical; if TRUE fit trend distribution instead of shuffling.

trend_dist

character vector of distributions for fitdistrplus::fitdist.

trend_ddt

step (seconds) for trend generation; defaults to 0.5/fs.

trend_alpha

p-value threshold to accept fit.

surrogate_method

surrogate generator; one of "auto", "event_shuffle", "event_trend", or "phase_randomized". In "auto" mode, continuous signals default to phase randomization, while event-like signals use shuffle or trend fitting depending on keep_trend.

signal_mode

one of "auto", "event", or "continuous".

warnings

logical; emit warnings.

fcor

logical; apply 1/f correction in surrogate peak estimation.

taper

taper applied before FFT; one of "none", "hann", "hanning".

Value

list with oscore_rp, fosc_rp, trendfit, surrogate_method, signal_mode, and (optionally) signrep.
